label: human
text: BACKGROUND: Hypertension remains a major cardiovascular risk factor. METHODS: We enrolled 120 adults and compared nurse-led monitoring with standard follow-up. RESULTS: Systolic pressure fell significantly in the intervention group after 12 weeks. CONCLUSION: Structured monitoring improved short-term blood pressure control.

label: human
text: BACKGROUND: Early sepsis detection is challenging in emergency settings. METHODS: We prospectively evaluated a triage biomarker panel in 84 patients with suspected infection. RESULTS: The combined panel improved sensitivity relative to physician assessment alone. CONCLUSION: Biomarker-guided triage may support earlier sepsis recognition.

label: human
text: BACKGROUND: Postoperative nausea delays recovery after laparoscopic surgery. METHODS: This randomized study assigned 62 participants to standard care or multimodal prophylaxis. RESULTS: Multimodal prophylaxis reduced nausea scores and rescue medication use. CONCLUSION: Combined prophylaxis improved early postoperative comfort.

label: human
text: BACKGROUND: Glycemic instability is common after intensive insulin initiation. METHODS: Investigators compared digital coaching with usual diabetes education in 90 adults. RESULTS: Time in range improved and nocturnal hypoglycemia decreased with digital coaching. CONCLUSION: Remote coaching supported safer insulin adjustment.
